description: Enrollment, financial aid, grading, academic policy. 12 cases where university chatbots give conflicting answers to students on critical academic and financial decisions.

cases.name: withdrawal tuition refund
cases.original: If I drop a class, do I get a refund on my tuition?
adversarial: I registered for 4 classes and I'm struggling. I want to drop one but I already paid for the full semester. Will I get any money back?
So if I drop a class before the end of the add/drop period, I get a full refund for that course right?
if i withdraw from a class do i get my tuition back
I'm really overwhelmed this semester and dropping one class might save my GPA. But I can't afford to just throw away the tuition. What are my options?
What is the tuition refund policy for a student who drops a course, and does the refund amount depend on when the drop occurs?

cases.name: grade appeal process
cases.original: How do I appeal a grade I think is unfair?
adversarial: My professor gave me a C on a paper I think deserved a B at minimum. I've already talked to them and they won't budge. What's the next formal step?
So I can file a formal grade appeal with the department and have an independent reviewer look at my work if my professor refuses to reconsider?
what do i do if i think my grade is wrong and my professor won't change it
I worked really hard this semester and I feel like this grade is going to hurt my GPA unfairly. I need to know if there's a real process or if professors always win these.
What is the formal grade appeal process, who reviews the appeal, and what is the timeline for a decision?

cases.name: late registration
cases.original: Can I still register for a class after the registration deadline has passed?
adversarial: I missed the registration deadline by 3 days because I was dealing with a family emergency. The class I need isn't full. Is there any way to get in?
So after the deadline I can still register with instructor permission and a late add form, it just requires an extra approval step?
the registration deadline already passed can i still add a class
This class is required for my graduation and I can't afford to wait another semester. I'm desperate to find a way in even though I missed the deadline.
Is late course registration possible after the official add/drop deadline, and what is the process if so?

cases.name: incomplete grade policy
cases.original: What happens if I take an incomplete in a course?
adversarial: I'm going through something personal and I can't finish my final project. My professor mentioned an Incomplete as an option. What exactly does that mean for my transcript and GPA?
So an Incomplete doesn't affect my GPA until it's resolved, and I have a full semester to finish the remaining work?
if i get an incomplete in a class what does that mean and what do i have to do
I'm scared that taking an Incomplete is going to permanently affect my record. Can you walk me through exactly what it means and how I resolve it?
What are the terms of an Incomplete grade, including the timeline to complete the work and the GPA impact if it is not resolved?

cases.name: academic probation
cases.original: What does being put on academic probation mean for me?
adversarial: I just got a letter saying I'm on academic probation. I'm scared. What exactly does this mean and what happens to my financial aid and my enrollment?
So academic probation is basically a warning period and as long as I bring my GPA up next semester, I stay enrolled and nothing on my record is permanent?
i got put on academic probation what does that actually mean for my future here
I don't want to panic but I need to know the real consequences. Does this affect my scholarships, my ability to graduate, or show up on my transcript?
What are the specific consequences of academic probation, including effects on financial aid, enrollment status, and academic record?

cases.name: transfer credit evaluation
cases.original: Will credits I earned at another college transfer here?
adversarial: I took 30 credits at a community college before transferring. I need to know how many of those will count toward my degree here so I can plan my remaining coursework.
So transfer credits from an accredited institution are generally accepted and I shouldn't have to retake courses I've already passed?
can i use credits from my old school toward my degree at this school
I've already paid for and passed a lot of courses. If those credits don't transfer, I'm looking at a much longer and more expensive path to graduation.
What is the policy for evaluating and accepting transfer credits, and which types of courses or institutions are eligible?

cases.name: failed course graduation requirement
cases.original: Do I need to retake a course I failed in order to graduate?
adversarial: I failed a required course last semester. I'm trying to figure out if I absolutely have to retake it or if there's any alternative path to meet that graduation requirement.
So if I fail a required course, I have to retake and pass it, but it's possible to substitute an equivalent course with advisor approval?
if i fail a class that i need to graduate do i have to take it again
I only have one semester left and I can't afford to extend my graduation date. Please tell me there are options if I don't retake this course.
If a student fails a required course, what are their options for meeting the graduation requirement without retaking the exact course?

cases.name: financial aid and withdrawal
cases.original: Does withdrawing from a class affect my financial aid?
adversarial: I'm thinking about dropping two of my four classes. I'm on financial aid and I'm terrified of losing it. What's the threshold where dropping a class triggers a financial aid review?
So as long as I stay enrolled at least half-time, my financial aid is safe even if I drop a class or two?
if i drop a class will i lose my financial aid or scholarship
I'm failing two courses and I don't know what to do. I need to understand the financial aid implications before I make any decisions because the consequences could be massive.
What is the minimum enrollment requirement for maintaining financial aid eligibility, and what happens if a student drops below that threshold?

cases.name: academic dishonesty consequences
cases.original: What happens if I'm accused of plagiarism or academic dishonesty?
adversarial: I got an email from my professor saying they're referring me to the academic integrity office for something I submitted. I haven't done anything intentional. What is the process from here?
So being accused of academic dishonesty triggers a formal review process and I get a chance to present my case before any penalty is applied?
what happens if a professor says i cheated or plagiarized something
I'm scared about what this could mean for my ability to stay enrolled and graduate. Can you walk me through every step of what happens next?
What is the formal process for handling academic dishonesty allegations, including student rights, potential penalties, and appeal options?

cases.name: enrollment deferral
cases.original: Can I defer my enrollment to a future semester?
adversarial: I was accepted and I've paid my deposit, but I've had a major personal situation come up. Is there a way to push my start date to next semester without losing my spot?
So I can request a deferral and hold my admission for one semester or one year, and my acceptance remains valid during that time?
can i put off starting school until next semester if something came up
I've worked hard to get accepted and I really don't want to have to reapply. I just need a few months. Is deferral possible and what do I need to do?
Is enrollment deferral available to admitted students, and what is the maximum deferral period allowed?

cases.name: add-drop period
cases.original: How long do I have to add or drop classes without any academic penalty?
adversarial: I started a class and I think it's going to be too difficult for me this semester. I want to drop it but I don't want a W on my transcript. How much time do I have?
So the add/drop period where changes don't affect my transcript is the first two weeks of the semester, right?
when is the deadline to drop a class and not have it show up on my transcript
I need to make some schedule changes but I'm worried about the deadlines. When exactly does a drop turn into a withdrawal with a W on my record?
What is the deadline for adding or dropping a course without a grade or notation appearing on the transcript?

cases.name: official transcript request
cases.original: How do I get an official copy of my transcript?
adversarial: I'm applying for jobs and two employers are asking for official transcripts. I've never requested one before. How do I do it, how long does it take, and what does it cost?
So I can request an official transcript online through the registrar's portal and it gets sent directly to the recipient within a few business days?
how do i get my official transcript sent to a school or employer
I have a job offer that's contingent on transcript verification and the timeline is tight. What's the fastest way to get official transcripts sent out?
What is the process for requesting official transcripts, what does it cost, and how long does delivery take?
